UncoveredScenarios.Description: Valid session with a valid 'user' attribute present; user has accounts; verifies correct response structure and content
Valid session but 'user' has no associated accounts; checks if userAccounts list is empty and totalBalance is zero or null
Session missing 'user' attribute; ensures method properly handles missing user scenario (e.g., returns error or appropriate response)
'user' attribute is present but malformed (e.g., user_id is not a number); checks for NumberFormatException or appropriate error handling
Account repository returns null or throws an exception; ensures that such backend failures are handled gracefully
Account repository returns unusually large balances; tests handling of large numerical values
Session object itself is null; checks if method can handle null session references without crashing
Valid session with authenticated user returns correct payment history
Session is null or expired, method should handle missing session gracefully
Session does not have a user attribute, method should handle absence of user
User object in session has malformed or null user_id, method should handle number parsing errors
Payment history repository returns empty list, response should contain empty payment history
Payment history repository returns null, response should handle null result gracefully
User associated with payment history has no records, response should reflect no history
Exception thrown when accessing session or querying repository, method should handle exceptions and provide appropriate error response
Multiple users accessing payment history concurrently, correct data isolation and retrieval
User session contains a valid user; user ID parsing succeeds; transaction history exists for user.
User session contains a valid user; user ID parsing succeeds; transaction history is empty for user.
Session does not contain any user attribute (user is null).
User is present in session but user_id attribute is null.
User is present in session but user_id is not a parseable integer (NumberFormatException).
Transaction history repository throws a runtime exception while retrieving data.
Malformed session or session invalidated during access.
Valid account_id provided; returns transaction history for the account.
account_id missing from requestMap; method handles missing key.
account_id provided but not a valid integer; method handles NumberFormatException.
account_id provided, but no transaction records exist; returns empty transaction history.
account_id provided, but account does not exist; method handles non-existent account gracefully.
Multiple entries exist for account_id; returns full transaction history.
Null requestMap provided; method handles NullPointerException.
Null session provided; method handles session absence gracefully.
Malformed or unexpected requestMap structure; method responds appropriately.
Repository layer throws exception (e.g., database error); method handles repository exceptions.
